poems.title: Sometimes
poems.author: Tarriana "Tank" Ball
lines: Sometimes
I'm afraid to hold on
Because I'm afraid to let go
poems.source: Featured in original Words Unlocked Lesson 1

poems.title: Harlem
poems.author: Langston Hughes
lines: What happens to a dream deferred?
Does it dry up like a raisin in the sun?
Or fester like a sore—
And then run?
Does it stink like rotten meat?
Or crust and sugar over—
like a syrupy sweet?
Maybe it just sags
like a heavy load.
Or does it explode?
poems.source: Featured in original Words Unlocked Lesson 2

poems.title: Still I Rise (excerpt)
poems.author: Maya Angelou
lines: You may write me down in history
With your bitter, twisted lies,
You may trod me in the very dirt
But still, like dust, I'll rise.
Just like moons and like suns,
With the certainty of tides,
Just like hopes springing high,
Still I'll rise.
poems.source: Commonly taught; rights status to verify before printing

poems.title: Sympathy
poems.author: Paul Laurence Dunbar
lines: I know what the caged bird feels, alas!
When the sun is bright on the upland slopes;
When the wind stirs soft through the springing grass,
And the river flows like a stream of glass;
When the first bird sings and the first bud opes,
And the faint perfume from its chalice steals—
I know what the caged bird feels!

poems.title: Invictus
poems.author: William Ernest Henley
lines: Out of the night that covers me,
Black as the pit from pole to pole,
I thank whatever gods may be
For my unconquerable soul.
In the fell clutch of circumstance
I have not winced nor cried aloud.
Under the bludgeonings of chance
My head is bloody, but unbowed.

poems.title: "Hope" is the thing with feathers
poems.author: Emily Dickinson
lines: "Hope" is the thing with feathers -
That perches in the soul -
And sings the tune without the words -
And never stops - at all -

poems.title: The Road Not Taken (excerpt)
poems.author: Robert Frost
lines: Two roads diverged in a yellow wood,
And sorry I could not travel both
And be one traveler, long I stood
And looked down one as far as I could
To where it bent in the undergrowth;
poems.source: 1916 — public domain in US but verify printing rights

poems.title: Song of Myself, 1 (excerpt)
poems.author: Walt Whitman
lines: I celebrate myself, and sing myself,
And what I assume you shall assume,
For every atom belonging to me as good belongs to you.

poems.title: What the Hand Releases
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: There is a weight you do not name,
the kind your knuckles learn before your tongue does.
Open your hand.
Watch what falls is not your name.
Watch what stays is not your shame.
Open your hand.
The sky is what you make of it.
poems.source: Original sample poem for theme alignment

poems.title: Forecast
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: Today I forgive the rain.
Yesterday I forgave the sun.
Tomorrow there will be a sky
I have not yet learned the name of.
I will know it
the way I have learned to know my own breath:
by losing it, and finding it again.
poems.source: Original sample poem for theme alignment

poems.title: My Phone
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: My phone is a window I keep cleaning.
My phone is a mirror I keep angry.
My phone is a door that opens only outward.
My phone is a friend with a quiet name.

poems.title: Recess
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: The bell rings like a starter pistol.
The hallway moves like a river that knows where it's going.
I run like a sentence with no period.
I land like a question someone asked twice.

poems.title: The Streetlight
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: The streetlight clears its throat at dusk
and tells its same old joke to the moths.
The moths laugh in tiny circles.
Nobody but me is listening.

poems.title: Bricks
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: Brother breaks the brick, builds the bridge,
borrows back the boy he used to be.
Brave is not a building. Brave is the brick.
Brave is the boy, building.

poems.title: Key
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: I keep an old key in my pocket.
It does not open any door I still own.
I keep it anyway.
Some locks live inside you long after the house is gone.

poems.title: Block Party
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: Boom — the speakers learn a new word.
Crackle — the foil unwraps the chicken.
Pop — the can confesses.
Shh — the baby agrees with the music.

poems.title: First Visit
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: The waiting room smells like vending machine coffee and old magazines.
The lights buzz like a forgotten promise.
My mother's coat is the color of a stop sign in rain.
She brought me a sandwich I can't taste.

poems.title: Mic Check
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: One. Two. One. Two.
If you can hear me — I have been quiet for a long time.
If you can hear me — I am still here.
If you can hear me — I am not done.
Check. Check. Check.

poems.title: Practice
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: I am practicing the long sentence of myself.
I am practicing the comma where I used to put the period.
I am practicing the question mark
in the middle of my own name.
Tomorrow I will practice the answer.

poems.title: Court
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: The court is a country I learned to read in.
The ball is a sentence I taught my hands to finish.
The hoop is a small mouth that says yes when I am honest.
The buzzer is a teacher who never grades on a curve.

poems.title: Sunday
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: My grandmother's hands smell like onion and Pine-Sol.
My grandmother's hands have buried two husbands and three brothers.
My grandmother's hands have never stopped cooking.
My grandmother's hands are the recipe.

poems.title: Kitchen Radio
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: The kitchen radio remembers the words my mother forgot.
It is older than my brother and twice as patient.
It hums the way pots do before they boil.
Nothing in this house is louder than its love.

poems.title: Subway
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: The train arrives like an argument finally over.
We board like a question the city forgot to ask.
The lights flicker like a song nobody requested.
We arrive somewhere our parents never named.

poems.title: Seedling
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: I broke through dirt before I had a name.
I bent toward light before I knew it was light.
I am still bending.
I am still becoming a kind of green I have not been before.

poems.title: What I Am Practicing
poems.author: BreakFree (sample)
lines: Forgiveness is a small door.
I keep walking past it.
Today I stopped.
Today I did not open it,
but I touched the handle, and the handle did not burn.
